one_time_description: Restatement to recognize fishing licenses as non-financial intangible assets, resulting in $11,510,189 added to accumulated surplus retrospectively
economic_notes: Band operates Crane Cove Seafoods Limited Partnership (99.99% share, commercial fishery), Eskasoni Supermarket Limited (wholly owned), 3312394 Nova Scotia Limited (wholly owned), Truro Heights Wind LP (25%), Eskasoni Kiptu Security Limited (51%), Eskasoni Cold Logistics Limited (50%), Live Store Sydney Limited (50%). Unincorporated enterprises include Eskasoni Tobacco Shop, Eskasoni Gaming Centre, Eskasoni Gaming Commission, Eskasoni Recreation Centre, Sarah Denny Cultural Centre, Eskasoni Fitness Centre. COVID-19 pandemic expenses of $777,049 recorded.
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2020 (not 2024). Auditor firm name not explicitly stated - signed 'Chartered Professional Accountants' from Sydney, Nova Scotia, May 26, 2021 (late audit). Annual surplus of $2,989,262 includes $5,418,121 earnings from Crane Cove Seafoods LP accounted via modified equity method. Major restatement in year: fishing licenses of $11,510,189 recognized retrospectively as intangible assets with corresponding increase to accumulated surplus. COVID-19 costs of $777,049 recorded. Long-term debt increased significantly with new RBC loan of $5,989,432. Housing expenses include Section 95 CMHC housing ($982,631) and other housing/infrastructure. Expenses_housing combines Housing and infrastructure ($3,257,793) plus CMHC Section 95 housing ($982,631). Net debt position of -$5,580,881 (net debt). Accumulated surplus per statement of change: $79,324,915. Chief and Council honorariums totaling $737,000 noted in related party transactions but no individual remuneration schedule provided.
